core_mechanism: Temporal/spatial event-coding of information into the timing structure of discrete pulse trains (rate, phase, burst, and first-event-latency modulation formats) injected into a physical dynamical medium, with a readout that decodes/classifies the medium's response, and the encoding format optimized to maximize class separability in a closed feedback loop
brief_reason: The abstract's whole method rests on comparing how inputs are modulated into stimulation-pulse timing patterns (rate-, phase-, burst-, and time-to-first-spike codes) delivered spatially and then decoded by a classifier, finding that the choice of temporal/spatial code determines decodability.
